methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.system.host.app.BaseLinuxApplication.run_cmds
documentation: Commands to run on node.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.system.host.app.BaseLinuxApplication.cleanup_cmds
documentation: Commands to run to cleanup node.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.system.host.app.BaseLinuxApplication.config_files
documentation:
Additional files to put inside the node, which are mounted under
`/tmp/guest/`.

Specified in the following format: `filename_inside_node`:
`IO_handle_of_file`

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.system.host.app.BaseLinuxApplication.prepare_pre_cp
documentation: Commands to run to prepare node before checkpointing.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.system.host.app.BaseLinuxApplication.prepare_post_cp
documentation: Commands to run to prepare node after checkpoint restore.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.system.host.app.BaseLinuxApplication.strfile
documentation:
Helper function to convert a string to an IO handle for usage in
`config_files()`.

Using this, you can create a file with the string as its content on the
simulated node.
Note AK: Maybe we can factor most of the duplicate calls with the host out
into a separate module.

functions.name: simbricks.orchestration.helpers.simulation.simple_simulation
documentation:
Create simple simulation from system. Uses a map from component type to
simulator type and then creates one simulator per component.

classes.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulator

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulator.get_unique_latency_period_sync
documentation: helper method for simulators that do not support
multiple sync periods etc. Should become eventually
at some point in the future...

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulator.resreq_cores
documentation:
Number of cores this simulator requires during execution.

This is used for scheduling multiple runs and experiments.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulator.resreq_mem
documentation:
Number of memory in MB this simulator requires during execution.

This is used for scheduling multiple runs and experiments.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulator.full_name
documentation: Full name of the simulator.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulator.run_cmd
documentation: Command to execute this simulator.
pylint: disable=unused-argument

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulator.sockets_cleanup
documentation: Sockets to be cleaned up: always the CONNECTING sockets
pylint: disable=unused-argument

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulator.sockets_wait
documentation: sockets to wait for indicating the simulator is ready
pylint: disable=unused-argument
Base class for all simulators.

classes.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulation

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulation.add_spec_sim_map
documentation: Add a mapping from specification to simulation instance

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulation.resreq_mem
documentation: Memory required to run all simulators in this experiment.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulation.resreq_cores
documentation: Number of Cores required to run all simulators in this experiment.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.simulation.base.Simulation.find_sim
documentation: Returns the used simulator object for the system component.

Base class for all simulation experiments.

Contains the simulators to be run and experiment-wide parameters.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.instantiation.base.Instantiation.create_checkpoint
documentation:
Whether to use checkpoint and restore for simulators.

The most common use-case for this is accelerating host simulator startup
by first running in a less accurate mode, then checkpointing the system
state after boot and running simulations from there.

methods.name: simbricks.orchestration.instantiation.base.Instantiation.join_imgs_path
documentation: def join_tmp_base(self, relative_path: str) -> str:
return self._join_paths(
base=self.tmp_dir(),
relative_path=relative_path,
